HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

6#include "DomainHom3D.h"
7#include <iostream>
8#include "Dipole.h"
9#include "IncidentField.h"
10#include "PlaneWave.h"
11#include "SIEFormPMCHW.h"
12#include "iofunctions.h"
13#include "Gaussian.h"

15// -----------------------------
16// Construction & lifetime
17// -----------------------------
18DomainHom3D::DomainHom3D(SurfaceMesh *inMesh, int inDIndex, dcmplx inEpsilon,
19 dcmplx inMu, dcmplx inVacuumWavelength)
20 : Domain(inMesh, inDIndex, inVacuumWavelength),
21 epsilon(inEpsilon),
22 mu(inMu) {
23 InitGrnFun();
24}
25
26DomainHom3D::DomainHom3D(SurfaceMesh *inMesh, int inDIndex, dcmplx inEpsilon,
27 dcmplx inMu, dcmplx inVacuumWavelength, bool parent)
28 : Domain(inMesh, inDIndex, inVacuumWavelength),
29 epsilon(inEpsilon),
30 mu(inMu) {
31 if (!parent) InitGrnFun();
32}
33
34DomainHom3D::~DomainHom3D() { delete grnFun; }
35
36// -----------------------------
37// Green's function & formulation
38// -----------------------------
39int DomainHom3D::InitGrnFun() {
40 grnFun = new GreenFHom3D(vacuumWavelength, epsilon, mu);
41 return 0;
42}
43
44int DomainHom3D::InitFormulation(dcmplx inVacWavelength) {
45 formulation = new SIEFormPMCHW(&rwgFuns, grnFun, &incidentFields,
46 inVacWavelength, epsilon, mu);
47 return 0;
48}
49
50// -----------------------------
51// Cross‑sections
52// -----------------------------
58cvec conjVecHom(cvec in) { return cvec(conj(in(0)), conj(in(1)), conj(in(2))); }
59
65rvec realVecHom(cvec in) { return rvec(real(in(0)), real(in(1)), real(in(2))); }
66
72std::vector<double> DomainHom3D::scatteringCS(blitz::Array<dcmplx,1>* solVector)
73{
74 // Gather unique triangles & normals
75 std::vector<Triangle*> vTri;
76 std::vector<rvec> vNormals;
77 vTri.reserve(rwgFuns.size());
78 vNormals.reserve(rwgFuns.size());
79
80 for (RWGFun& rwg : rwgFuns) {
81 Triangle* T = rwg.TrianglePtr();
82 auto it = std::find(vTri.begin(), vTri.end(), T);
83 if (it == vTri.end()) {
84 vTri.push_back(T);
85 vNormals.push_back(T->FBNormalVector(rwg.Side()));
86 }
87 }
88
89 size_t Ntri = vTri.size(); // number of unique triangles
90
91 // Quadrature rule on reference triangle
92 int nQuad = dunavant::N5; // number of quadrature points
93 auto& xQuad = dunavant::x5; // barycentrics [nQuad][3]
94 auto& wQuad = dunavant::w5; // weights[nQuad]
95
96 // Allocate per-triangle, per-quad-point accumulators
97 std::vector<std::vector<cvec>> Etot(Ntri, std::vector<cvec>(nQuad, cvec(0, 0, 0))),
98 Htot(Ntri, std::vector<cvec>(nQuad, cvec(0, 0, 0)));
99
100 int offset = solVector->shape()(0) / 2; // offset for β coefficients
101
102 // Fill E, H normal and tangential components at each sample point
103 for (RWGFun& rwg : rwgFuns) {
104 // find which triangle index
105 Triangle* T = rwg.TrianglePtr();
106 size_t triIndex = std::distance(vTri.begin(),
107 std::find(vTri.begin(), vTri.end(), T));
108 rvec normal = vNormals[triIndex];
109
110 // RWG coefficients
111 dcmplx alpha = (*solVector)(rwg.EdgeIndex());
112 dcmplx beta = (*solVector)(rwg.EdgeIndex() + offset);
113 double divf = rwg.RWGPreFactor();
114
115 // triangle nodes
116 rvec r[3] = { T->Node(0), T->Node(1), T->Node(2) };
117
118 // loop quad points
119 for (int q = 0; q < nQuad; ++q) {
120 // physical sample point
121 rvec s = xQuad[q][0] * r[0] + xQuad[q][1] * r[1] + xQuad[q][2] * r[2];
122
123 // hRWG at s
124 rvec fval = rwg.Evaluate(s);
125
126 // normal‐component (constant over tri)
127 cvec En_contrib = -I * alpha * divf * normal / (omega * epsilon * EPS0);
128 cvec Hn_contrib = -I * beta * divf * normal / (omega * mu * MU0);
129
130 // tangential‐component
131 cvec Et_contrib = cross( (cvec)normal, (cvec)(beta * fval));
132 cvec Ht_contrib = -cross( (cvec)normal, (cvec)(alpha * fval));
133
134 // J,M currents have contributions from each RWG with opposite sign, thus -=
135 Etot[triIndex][q] -= En_contrib + Et_contrib;
136 Htot[triIndex][q] -= Hn_contrib + Ht_contrib;
137 }
138 }
139
140 // Quadrature - find the scattering cross section
141 double sigma = 0.0;
142 double near = 0.0;
143 for (size_t t = 0; t < Ntri; ++t) {
144 rvec normal = vNormals[t];
145 double area = vTri[t]->Area();
146
147 // re-compute nodes for this triangle
148 rvec r[3] = { vTri[t]->Node(0), vTri[t]->Node(1), vTri[t]->Node(2) };
149
150 for (int q = 0; q < nQuad; ++q) {
151 // map barycentrics -> point
152 rvec s = xQuad[q][0] * r[0] + xQuad[q][1] * r[1] + xQuad[q][2] * r[2];
153
154 cvec Einc = IncFieldE(s);
155 cvec Hinc = IncFieldH(s);
156 rvec Sinc = realVecHom( cross(Einc, conjVecHom(Hinc)) ) / 2.0;
157
158 cvec Escat = Etot[t][q] - Einc;
159 cvec Hscat = Htot[t][q] - Hinc;
160 rvec Sscat = realVecHom( cross(Escat, conjVecHom(Hscat)) ) / 2.0;
161
162 sigma += wQuad[q] * area * dot(normal, Sscat) / sqrt( dot( Sinc, Sinc ) );
163 near += real( wQuad[q] * area * dot( Escat, conjVecHom(Escat) ) );
164 }
165 }
166 return {sigma, near};
167}
168
172double DomainHom3D::absorptionCS(blitz::Array<dcmplx,1>* solVector)
173{
174 // Gather unique triangles & normals
175 std::vector<Triangle*> vTri;
176 std::vector<rvec> vNormals;
177 vTri.reserve(rwgFuns.size());
178 vNormals.reserve(rwgFuns.size());
179
180 for (RWGFun& rwg : rwgFuns) {
181 Triangle* T = rwg.TrianglePtr();
182 auto it = std::find(vTri.begin(), vTri.end(), T);
183 if (it == vTri.end()) {
184 vTri.push_back(T);
185 vNormals.push_back(T->FBNormalVector(rwg.Side()));
186 }
187 }
188
189 size_t Ntri = vTri.size(); // number of unique triangles
190
191 // Quadrature rule on reference triangle
192 int nQuad = dunavant::N5; // number of quadrature points
193 auto& xQuad = dunavant::x5; // barycentrics [nQuad][3]
194 auto& wQuad = dunavant::w5; // weights[nQuad]
195
196 // Allocate per-triangle, per-quad-point accumulators
197 std::vector<std::vector<cvec>> Etot(Ntri, std::vector<cvec>(nQuad, cvec(0, 0, 0))),
198 Htot(Ntri, std::vector<cvec>(nQuad, cvec(0, 0, 0)));
199
200 int offset = solVector->shape()(0) / 2; // offset for β coefficients
201
202 // Fill E, H normal and tangential components at each sample point
203 for (RWGFun& rwg : rwgFuns) {
204 // find which triangle index
205 Triangle* T = rwg.TrianglePtr();
206 size_t triIndex = std::distance(vTri.begin(),
207 std::find(vTri.begin(), vTri.end(), T));
208 rvec normal = vNormals[triIndex];
209
210 // RWG coefficients
211 dcmplx alpha = (*solVector)(rwg.EdgeIndex());
212 dcmplx beta = (*solVector)(rwg.EdgeIndex() + offset);
213 double divf = rwg.RWGPreFactor();
214
215 // triangle nodes
216 rvec r[3] = { T->Node(0), T->Node(1), T->Node(2) };
217
218 // loop quad points
219 for (int q = 0; q < nQuad; ++q) {
220 // physical sample point
221 rvec s = xQuad[q][0] * r[0] + xQuad[q][1] * r[1] + xQuad[q][2] * r[2];
222
223 // hRWG at s
224 rvec fval = rwg.Evaluate(s);
225
226 // normal‐component (constant over tri)
227 cvec En_contrib = -I * alpha * divf * normal / (omega * epsilon * EPS0);
228 cvec Hn_contrib = -I * beta * divf * normal / (omega * mu * MU0);
229
230 // tangential‐component
231 cvec Et_contrib = cross( (cvec)normal, (cvec)(beta * fval));
232 cvec Ht_contrib = -cross( (cvec)normal, (cvec)(alpha * fval));
233
234 // J,M currents have contributions from each RWG with opposite sign, thus -=
235 Etot[triIndex][q] -= En_contrib + Et_contrib;
236 Htot[triIndex][q] -= Hn_contrib + Ht_contrib;
237 }
238 }
239
240 // Quadrature - find the scattering cross section
241 double sigma = 0.0;
242 for (size_t t = 0; t < Ntri; ++t) {
243 rvec normal = vNormals[t];
244 double area = vTri[t]->Area();
245
246 // re-compute nodes for this triangle
247 rvec r[3] = { vTri[t]->Node(0), vTri[t]->Node(1), vTri[t]->Node(2) };
248
249 for (int q = 0; q < nQuad; ++q) {
250 // map barycentrics -> point
251 rvec s = xQuad[q][0] * r[0] + xQuad[q][1] * r[1] + xQuad[q][2] * r[2];
252
253 cvec Einc = IncFieldE(s);
254 cvec Hinc = IncFieldH(s);
255 rvec Sinc = realVecHom( cross(Einc, conjVecHom(Hinc)) ) / 2.0;
256
257 cvec E = Etot[t][q];
258 cvec H = Htot[t][q];
259 rvec S = realVecHom( cross(E, conjVecHom(H)) ) / 2.0;
260
261 sigma -= wQuad[q] * area * dot(normal, S) / sqrt( dot( Sinc, Sinc ) );
262 }
263 }
264 return sigma;
265}
266
270double DomainHom3D::extinctionCS(blitz::Array<dcmplx,1>* solVector)
271{
272 // Gather unique triangles & normals
273 std::vector<Triangle*> vTri;
274 std::vector<rvec> vNormals;
275 vTri.reserve(rwgFuns.size());
276 vNormals.reserve(rwgFuns.size());
277
278 for (RWGFun& rwg : rwgFuns) {
279 Triangle* T = rwg.TrianglePtr();
280 auto it = std::find(vTri.begin(), vTri.end(), T);
281 if (it == vTri.end()) {
282 vTri.push_back(T);
283 vNormals.push_back(T->FBNormalVector(rwg.Side()));
284 }
285 }
286
287 size_t Ntri = vTri.size(); // number of unique triangles
288
289 // Quadrature rule on reference triangle
290 int nQuad = dunavant::N5; // number of quadrature points
291 auto& xQuad = dunavant::x5; // barycentrics [nQuad][3]
292 auto& wQuad = dunavant::w5; // weights[nQuad]
293
294 // Allocate per-triangle, per-quad-point accumulators
295 std::vector<std::vector<cvec>> Etot(Ntri, std::vector<cvec>(nQuad, cvec(0, 0, 0))),
296 Htot(Ntri, std::vector<cvec>(nQuad, cvec(0, 0, 0)));
297
298 int offset = solVector->shape()(0) / 2; // offset for β coefficients
299
300 // Fill E, H normal and tangential components at each sample point
301 for (RWGFun& rwg : rwgFuns) {
302 // find which triangle index
303 Triangle* T = rwg.TrianglePtr();
304 size_t triIndex = std::distance(vTri.begin(),
305 std::find(vTri.begin(), vTri.end(), T));
306 rvec normal = vNormals[triIndex];
307
308 // RWG coefficients
309 dcmplx alpha = (*solVector)(rwg.EdgeIndex());
310 dcmplx beta = (*solVector)(rwg.EdgeIndex() + offset);
311 double divf = rwg.RWGPreFactor();
312
313 // triangle nodes
314 rvec r[3] = { T->Node(0), T->Node(1), T->Node(2) };
315
316 // loop quad points
317 for (int q = 0; q < nQuad; ++q) {
318 // physical sample point
319 rvec s = xQuad[q][0] * r[0] + xQuad[q][1] * r[1] + xQuad[q][2] * r[2];
320
321 // hRWG at s
322 rvec fval = rwg.Evaluate(s);
323
324 // normal‐component (constant over tri)
325 cvec En_contrib = -I * alpha * divf * normal / (omega * epsilon * EPS0);
326 cvec Hn_contrib = -I * beta * divf * normal / (omega * mu * MU0);
327
328 // tangential‐component
329 cvec Et_contrib = cross( (cvec)normal, (cvec)(beta * fval));
330 cvec Ht_contrib = -cross( (cvec)normal, (cvec)(alpha * fval));
331
332 // J,M currents have contributions from each RWG with opposite sign, thus -=
333 Etot[triIndex][q] -= En_contrib + Et_contrib;
334 Htot[triIndex][q] -= Hn_contrib + Ht_contrib;
335 }
336 }
337
338 // Quadrature - find the scattering cross section
339 double sigma = 0.0;
340 for (size_t t = 0; t < Ntri; ++t) {
341 rvec normal = vNormals[t];
342 double area = vTri[t]->Area();
343
344 // re-compute nodes for this triangle
345 rvec r[3] = { vTri[t]->Node(0), vTri[t]->Node(1), vTri[t]->Node(2) };
346
347 for (int q = 0; q < nQuad; ++q) {
348 // map barycentrics -> point
349 rvec s = xQuad[q][0] * r[0] + xQuad[q][1] * r[1] + xQuad[q][2] * r[2];
350
351 cvec Einc = IncFieldE(s);
352 cvec Hinc = IncFieldH(s);
353 rvec Sinc = realVecHom( cross(Einc, conjVecHom(Hinc)) ) / 2.0;
354
355 cvec Escat = Etot[t][q] - Einc;
356 cvec Hscat = Htot[t][q] - Hinc;
357 rvec Sext = realVecHom( cross(Einc, conjVecHom(Hscat)) +
358 cross(Escat, conjVecHom(Hinc)) ) / 2.0;
359
360 sigma -= wQuad[q] * area * dot(normal, Sext) / sqrt( dot( Sinc, Sinc ) );
361 }
362 }
363 return sigma;
364}
365
366// -----------------------------
367// Material properties & layered stubs
368// -----------------------------
369dcmplx DomainHom3D::Epsilon(rvec /*pos*/) { return epsilon; }
370
371dcmplx DomainHom3D::Mu(rvec /*pos*/) { return mu; }
372
373void DomainHom3D::newGrnFunLayered(std::vector<rvec>& /*posvec*/) {
374 throw std::runtime_error("Not implemented for homogeneous domains.");
375}
376
377// -----------------------------
378// Incident field factories
379// -----------------------------
380int DomainHom3D::AddPlaneWave(dcmplx wavelength, rvec propagationDirection,
381 cvec polarization) {
382 IncidentField *ptr = new PlaneWave(wavelength, real(epsilon),
383 propagationDirection, polarization);
384 incidentFields.push_back(ptr);
385 return 0;
386}
387
388int DomainHom3D::AddDipole(rvec location, cvec polarization) {
389 IncidentField *ptr =
390 new Dipole(location, polarization, vacuumWavelength, mu, grnFun);
391 incidentFields.push_back(ptr);
392 return 0;
393}
394
395int DomainHom3D::AddGaussian(double wavelength, double waist, rvec focal_point,
396 rvec propagationDirection, cvec polarization) {
397 IncidentField *ptr =
398 new Gaussian(wavelength, waist, focal_point, real(epsilon),
399 propagationDirection, polarization);
400 incidentFields.push_back(ptr);
401 return 0;
402}
Dipole.h
Incident field generator for electric dipole illumination in homogeneous or layered media.

Extract the real part of each component of a complex 3-vector.
